descript.abstract: This dataset contains the detailed information about the individual asset point features such as recreation sites, that make up Great American Outdoors Act (GAOA) projects. This data can be used together with the project summary data to display general project and asset locations. The data is refreshed on a nightly basis from the US Forest Service database of infrastructure projects which is stewarded by the individual National Forests and Grasslands. For more information about Forest Service GAOA projects visit our website: https://www.fs.usda.gov/managing-land/gaoa
descript.purpose: This dataset informs and supports the National Asset Management Program (NAMP) process for evaluation and ranking of projects targeted for submission as Great American Outdoor Act (GAOA) projects. It is also used for accomplishment reporting.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

dataqual.logic: The spatial locations for the points in this dataset come from the USFS RecreationSite dataset (S_USA.RecreationSite).
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of publication.

procstep.procdesc: This dataset is created by pulling from the Infra NAMP Project Module, FMMI and other internal USFS databases of record.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: The longitude value of the individual feature, if applicable. Values of “0” in the Longitude field indicates that the asset coordinates are not available.

attrdomv.udom: Numerical value for the longitude of individual features

attr.attrdef: The system identified primary key for the route (road or trail)

The task CN is the system generated unique identifier for a task. A single asset in a project may be tied to numerous tasks, which will translate to a scenario where a single asset appears on multiple rows, each with a distinct task_cn. The task_cn field allows users to determine the total number of tasks associated with an asset.
The system identified primary key for the land unit (admin site or rec site)

attrdomv.udom: Primary key for the land unit

attr.attrdef: Anticipated start date (YYYY/MM/DD)
The latitude value of the administrative site or recreation site, if applicable. Values of “0” in the Latitude field indicates that the asset coordinates are not available.

attrdomv.udom: Numerical value for the administrative or recreation site latitude

attr.attrdef: The parent level asset type: Administrative Site, Recreation Site, (null).
The system ID for the individual feature
Anticipated finish date (YYYY/MM/DD)
The system name for the individual feature
Identifies individual work tasks as deferred maintenance or capital improvements
Actual finish date (YYYY/MM/DD)
The status of approval for the project

edom.edomvd: The Washington Office has approved and funded the project
The Forest Supervisor's Office has approved the project
The Regional Office has approved the project

attr.attrdef: Reads from the congressional values associated with the included assets. If multiple congressional districts are listed in the tabular data for a road or trail, multiple congressional districts are listed in this field
The longitude value of the administrative site or recreation site, if applicable. Values of “0” in the Longitude field indicates that the asset coordinates are not available.

attrdomv.udom: Numerical value for the administrative or recreation site longitude

attr.attrdef: Derived from the user entered Security ID in Infra where Region is represented by the first 2 digits

User-defined name for the project. For projects preloaded from the FY21 or FY22 GAOA list or the FY22 CIM list, the project names come from the NAMP project submissions and congressionally approved project lists. For new projects initiated in IPM the project name is defined by the user and can be edited.
The system identified primary key for the feature
Feature geometry.
State and district code base on the State and Congressional_District values
State and County FIPS code based on the State and County values
The system name for the parent level asset
Reads from the state values associated with the included assets. If multiple states are listed in the tabular data for a road or trail, multiple states are listed in this field
The system identified primary key for the project. Use the PROJ_CN to join to the PROJECT_CN in the Infra_NAMP_Project_Summary layer it is the unique record identifier.
The status of the project. If the project has been 'terminated', there should be an explanation in the remarks field.

edom.edomvd: The project work has been completed
The project has been stopped and is no longer under consideration
Project planning and preparation phases are complete and the project is ready for implementation
The project has been put on hold

attr.attrdef: The system ID for the parent level asset

If the project crosses district boundaries, user inputs only the forest level org number. If the project crosses two forests, user inputs the primary managing (lead) forest org number or enters the managing region org number
The latitude value of the individual feature, if applicable. Values of “0” in the Latitude field indicates that the asset coordinates are not available.

attrdomv.udom: Numerical value for the latitude of individual features

attr.attrdef: This is a flag for distinct assets to allow a user to count total number of ASSET_TYPES (i.e., number of roads, number of developed recreation sites, etc). Marked with a "Y".
Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Reads from the county values associated with the included assets. If multiple counties are listed in the tabular data for a road or trail, multiple counties are listed in this field

User-defined identifier for the project. It was standardized for FY21 (with a budget code) and FY22 (no budget code, just a number). Until a projects is selected and funded, it will not have a unique budget id.
Actual start date (YYYY/MM/DD)
The category of an individual feature associated with a parent asset.

Type of project. When a project gets congressional approval for GAOA funding, the NRM IPM admin changes the value of the Proj Type for that project to "GAOA". Only projects with congressionally approved funding get this project type.

edom.edomvd: Great American Outdoors Act - Legacy Restoration Fund

attr.attrdef: Currently Null. Coming soon. Populated from the NRM Contacts table based on the Region
The fiscal year the projected was selected and approved for national funding, whether through CIM, FLTP, or GAOA
Currently Null. Coming soon. Derived from the user entered Security ID in Infra where Region is represented by the first 2 digits
Currently Null. Coming soon. Populated from the NRM Contacts table based on the Security ID

distinfo.distliab: The U.S. Forest Service makes no warranty, express or implied, nor assumes any liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness, or utility of these geospatial data or for the improper or incorrect use of those data. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible for verifying the limitations of the geospatial data and for using the data accordingly.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
